title: HTML lex DFA extraction bug: state 76 doesn't stop at '<'
description: The generated HTML lex function has a bug in state 76 (and possibly others). State 76 loops on ALL non-EOF, non-whitespace characters without checking for '<'. It should stop at '<' but instead consumes closing tags like '' as part of the text content. This produces wrong parse trees (text instead of element nodes). This is a lex DFA extraction/generation bug in internal/generate/extract.go - the C parser.c lex function correctly handles '<' transitions but the Go extraction misses them. Root cause is likely in how the extractLex function translates the C switch/case DFA into Go.
